medical_history.family_history: Bone dysplasia was not known so far in the patient's family

physical_examination.general_examination: free mobility of the left hip joint with moderate pain-perception by the patient at the final point of internal rotation. Normal skin without local swelling was noted. Neurological examination of the leg and pulses were normal.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Histological investigation of the tumour showed an osteochondroma.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Conventional X-rays of the pelvis showed an asymmetrical ossification proceeding from the left acetabulum compared with joint incongruency and discreet lateralisation of the femoral head.
The CT of the pelvis showed an asymmetrical ossification in projection on the left acetabular roof located in the pubic portion.

Conventional X-rays after surgical removal of the ossification showed the femoral head centrally positioned in the left acetabulum. Furthermore, the left acetabulum showed a thick bottom compared with the right acetabulum, leading to a secondary dysplasia of the left hip.

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: CT of the left hip joint after arthrotomy
radiographic_examinations.findings: CT of the left hip joint after arthrotomy and open removal of the tumour showed the femoral head centrally positioned in the left acetabulum.

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Conventional X-rays 22 months after surgical removal
radiographic_examinations.findings: Conventional X-rays 22 months after surgical removal of the ossification showed the central position of the femoral head in the acetabulum which was partially uneven. Furthermore, the joint showed a secondary dysplasia caused by the thick bottom of the left acetabulum.

other_tests.findings: Clinical examination showed free mobility of the left hip joint with moderate pain-perception by the patient at the final point of internal rotation. Normal skin without local swelling was noted. Neurological examination of the leg and pulses were normal.
Four months after the investigation the patient showed normal gait. We found free mobility of the left hip joint whereby internal rotation of the left hip was slightly reduced.
Two years after the operation, the left hip range of motion was comparable to the situation 4 months after the investigation. We found a normal gait without any pain.
